---
title: "DEV_R036: ShinyJS Module Namespace Handling Rule"
subtitle: "When using shinyjs functions within moduleServer context, use session$ns() instead of ns() to properly namespace element IDs"
chapter: "CH03"
category: "rule"
number: "DEV_R036"
date-created: "2025-09-28"
date-modified: "2025-09-28"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "Development Methodology Law"
article-number:
  number: 36
  title: "ShinyJS Module Namespace Handling Rule"
related_to:
  - "UI_R002: Shiny Module ID Handling Rule"
  - "DEV_R030: Explicit Namespace in Shiny Applications Rule"
  - "DEV_R022: Module Data Connection Rule"
  - "MP017: Separation of Concerns"
  - "MP058: Namespace Conflict Avoidance"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 36: ShinyJS Module Namespace Handling Rule {#overview}

**Development Methodology Law Article 36 (ShinyJS Module Namespace Handling Rule)**

This rule establishes the correct pattern for using shinyjs functions within Shiny modules, specifically addressing the namespace function availability in different contexts.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule
When using shinyjs functions (show, hide, toggle, etc.) within `moduleServer` context, you MUST use `session$ns()` to namespace element IDs, not `ns()` which is only available in UI functions.
:::

# Section 1: The Problem {#problem}

## Subsection 1: Namespace Context Difference {#namespace-context}

In Shiny modules, there are two different namespace contexts:

1. **UI Context**: The `ns()` function created by `NS(id)` in UI functions
2. **Server Context**: The `session$ns()` function available in `moduleServer`

## Subsection 2: Common Error Pattern {#error-pattern}

The following error occurs when attempting to use `ns()` in server context:

```r
# INCORRECT - Causes Error
moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
  observeEvent(input$button, {
    shinyjs::show(id = ns("panel"))  # ❌ Error: could not find function "ns"
  })
})
```

This happens because `ns()` is not defined within the `moduleServer` function scope.

# Section 2: Implementation Guidelines {#implementation}

## Subsection 1: Correct Pattern in Module Server {#correct-server-pattern}

When using shinyjs functions within `moduleServer`, always use `session$ns()`:

```r
# CORRECT - Using session$ns()
moduleNameServer <- function(id) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    observeEvent(input$toggle_button, {
      # Use session$ns() to namespace the ID
      shinyjs::toggle(id = session$ns("panel"))
    })

    observeEvent(input$show_button, {
      shinyjs::show(id = session$ns("hidden_content"))
    })

    observeEvent(input$hide_button, {
      shinyjs::hide(id = session$ns("visible_content"))
    })
  })
}
```

## Subsection 2: UI Function Pattern Remains Unchanged {#ui-pattern}

In UI functions, continue using the standard `ns()` pattern:

```r
# CORRECT - Using ns() in UI function
moduleNameUI <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)  # Create namespace function

  tagList(
    shinyjs::useShinyjs(),

    # Use ns() for all IDs in UI
    actionButton(ns("toggle_button"), "Toggle Panel"),
    actionButton(ns("show_button"), "Show Content"),
    actionButton(ns("hide_button"), "Hide Content"),

    # Hidden elements also use ns()
    shinyjs::hidden(
      div(
        id = ns("panel"),  # This ID will be referenced as session$ns("panel") in server
        h3("Panel Content"),
        p("This panel can be toggled")
      )
    ),

    div(
      id = ns("hidden_content"),
      style = "display: none;",
      p("Initially hidden content")
    ),

    div(
      id = ns("visible_content"),
      p("Initially visible content")
    )
  )
}
```

# Section 3: Complete Working Example {#complete-example}

## Subsection 1: Full Module Implementation {#full-implementation}

```r
# UI Function
collapsiblePanelUI <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)

  tagList(
    shinyjs::useShinyjs(),

    bs4Dash::box(
      title = "Collapsible Panel",
      width = 12,

      # Toggle button
      actionButton(
        ns("toggle_btn"),
        "Toggle Content",
        class = "btn-primary mb-3"
      ),

      # Content to be toggled
      shinyjs::hidden(
        div(
          id = ns("collapsible_content"),
          class = "mt-3",

          # Multiple elements within the collapsible section
          selectInput(
            ns("selector"),
            "Select Option:",
            choices = c("Option A", "Option B", "Option C")
          ),

          textAreaInput(
            ns("notes"),
            "Notes:",
            rows = 3
          ),

          actionButton(
            ns("submit"),
            "Submit",
            class = "btn-success"
          )
        )
      ),

      # Status message
      div(
        id = ns("status_message"),
        class = "alert alert-info mt-3",
        style = "display: none;",
        textOutput(ns("status_text"))
      )
    )
  )
}

# Server Function
collapsiblePanelServer <- function(id) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {

    # Toggle panel visibility
    observeEvent(input$toggle_btn, {
      # CORRECT: Using session$ns() for shinyjs functions
      shinyjs::toggle(
        id = session$ns("collapsible_content"),
        anim = TRUE,
        animType = "slide"
      )
    })

    # Handle form submission
    observeEvent(input$submit, {
      # Show status message
      shinyjs::show(
        id = session$ns("status_message"),
        anim = TRUE
      )

      # Update status text
      output$status_text <- renderText({
        paste("Submitted:", input$selector, "with notes:", input$notes)
      })

      # Hide message after 3 seconds
      shinyjs::delay(
        3000,
        shinyjs::hide(
          id = session$ns("status_message"),
          anim = TRUE
        )
      )
    })

    # Conditional visibility based on selection
    observeEvent(input$selector, {
      if (input$selector == "Option C") {
        # Show submit button only for Option C
        shinyjs::show(id = session$ns("submit"))
      } else {
        shinyjs::hide(id = session$ns("submit"))
      }
    })
  })
}
```

# Section 4: Common ShinyJS Functions Requiring session$ns() {#shinyjs-functions}

## Subsection 1: Visibility Functions {#visibility-functions}

All shinyjs visibility functions require `session$ns()` when used in modules:

```r
# Inside moduleServer
shinyjs::show(id = session$ns("element_id"))
shinyjs::hide(id = session$ns("element_id"))
shinyjs::toggle(id = session$ns("element_id"))
shinyjs::toggleState(id = session$ns("button_id"))
shinyjs::enable(id = session$ns("input_id"))
shinyjs::disable(id = session$ns("input_id"))
```

## Subsection 2: CSS and Class Functions {#css-functions}

CSS manipulation functions also require proper namespacing:

```r
# Inside moduleServer
shinyjs::addClass(id = session$ns("element_id"), class = "highlight")
shinyjs::removeClass(id = session$ns("element_id"), class = "highlight")
shinyjs::toggleClass(id = session$ns("element_id"), class = "active")

# Inline CSS
shinyjs::inlineCSS(paste0("#", session$ns("element_id"), " { color: red; }"))
```

## Subsection 3: Advanced ShinyJS Functions {#advanced-functions}

More complex shinyjs operations:

```r
# Inside moduleServer

# Run JavaScript with namespaced IDs
shinyjs::runjs(sprintf(
  "document.getElementById('%s').scrollIntoView();",
  session$ns("target_element")
))

# HTML manipulation
shinyjs::html(
  id = session$ns("content_div"),
  html = "<p>New content</p>"
)

# Delayed actions
shinyjs::delay(
  1000,
  shinyjs::hide(id = session$ns("temporary_message"))
)

# Click simulation
shinyjs::click(id = session$ns("hidden_button"))
```

# Section 5: Anti-patterns to Avoid {#anti-patterns}

## Subsection 1: Creating ns Function in Server {#creating-ns-server}

:::{.callout-warning}
## Never Create NS() in moduleServer

```r
# INCORRECT - Do not create NS in server
moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
  ns <- NS(id)  # ❌ This creates a new namespace, not the module's namespace

  observeEvent(input$button, {
    shinyjs::show(id = ns("panel"))  # ❌ Wrong namespace
  })
})
```

This creates a new namespace function that doesn't match the module's actual namespace.
:::

## Subsection 2: Using Unnamespaced IDs {#unnamespaced-ids}

:::{.callout-warning}
## Always Namespace Module IDs

```r
# INCORRECT - Missing namespace
moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
  observeEvent(input$button, {
    shinyjs::show(id = "panel")  # ❌ Will not find the element in module context
  })
})
```

This will fail to find the element because it's looking for a global ID, not the module-namespaced ID.
:::

## Subsection 3: Mixed Namespace Approaches {#mixed-namespace}

:::{.callout-warning}
## Inconsistent Namespace Usage

```r
# INCORRECT - Inconsistent approach
moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
  # Creating local ns variable (confusing and error-prone)
  ns <- session$ns  # ❌ Unnecessary and confusing

  observeEvent(input$button, {
    shinyjs::show(id = ns("panel"))  # Works but unnecessarily complex
  })
})
```

While this technically works, it's unnecessarily complex and can lead to confusion. Always use `session$ns()` directly.
:::

# Section 6: Testing and Debugging {#testing}

## Subsection 1: Verifying Namespace {#verify-namespace}

To debug namespace issues, you can print the namespaced ID:

```r
moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
  observeEvent(input$debug_button, {
    # Print the actual namespaced ID
    print(paste("Namespaced ID:", session$ns("panel")))

    # Verify element exists
    shinyjs::runjs(sprintf(
      "console.log('Element exists:', document.getElementById('%s') !== null);",
      session$ns("panel")
    ))
  })
})
```

## Subsection 2: Common Debugging Steps {#debugging-steps}

1. **Check browser console**: Look for JavaScript errors about missing elements
2. **Verify ID matching**: Ensure UI uses `ns()` and server uses `session$ns()`
3. **Print namespaced IDs**: Use `print(session$ns("id"))` to see actual IDs
4. **Inspect HTML**: Use browser developer tools to see actual element IDs

# Section 7: Integration with Other Module Patterns {#integration}

## Subsection 1: Nested Modules {#nested-modules}

When modules are nested, each level maintains its own namespace:

```r
# Parent module server
parentModuleServer <- function(id) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    # Parent's shinyjs calls
    observeEvent(input$parent_button, {
      shinyjs::show(id = session$ns("parent_panel"))
    })

    # Call child module (no special namespace handling needed)
    childModuleServer("child")
  })
}

# Child module server
childModuleServer <- function(id) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    # Child's shinyjs calls use child's session$ns
    observeEvent(input$child_button, {
      shinyjs::show(id = session$ns("child_panel"))
    })
  })
}
```

## Subsection 2: Dynamic UI with ShinyJS {#dynamic-ui}

When creating dynamic UI that will be manipulated with shinyjs:

```r
moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
  output$dynamic_ui <- renderUI({
    tagList(
      actionButton(session$ns("dynamic_button"), "Dynamic Action"),
      shinyjs::hidden(
        div(
          id = session$ns("dynamic_content"),
          "This content can be toggled"
        )
      )
    )
  })

  # Manipulate dynamically created elements
  observeEvent(input$dynamic_button, {
    shinyjs::toggle(id = session$ns("dynamic_content"))
  })
})
```

# Implementation Checklist {#checklist}

:::{.callout-note}
## ShinyJS Module Namespace Checklist

When using shinyjs in modules, verify:

- [ ] UI function uses `ns <- NS(id)` and `ns()` for all element IDs
- [ ] Server function uses `moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {...})`
- [ ] All shinyjs function calls use `session$ns("element_id")` in server
- [ ] No `NS()` function is created inside `moduleServer`
- [ ] Dynamic UI elements use `session$ns()` when created in `renderUI`
- [ ] Nested modules each use their own `session$ns()`
- [ ] JavaScript code snippets properly escape namespaced IDs
- [ ] Error messages about missing functions have been resolved
- [ ] Browser console shows no errors about missing elements
- [ ] Module works correctly when used multiple times with different IDs
:::

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

The ShinyJS Module Namespace Handling Rule ensures that shinyjs functions work correctly within Shiny modules by using the appropriate namespace function in each context. Remember:

- **In UI functions**: Use `ns()` created by `NS(id)`
- **In server functions**: Use `session$ns()` provided by `moduleServer`
- **Never**: Create `NS()` inside `moduleServer` or use unnamespaced IDs

Following this pattern prevents the common "could not find function 'ns'" error and ensures your modules work reliably regardless of how they're instantiated or nested.

---

*Based on: Development Methodology Law Article 36*
*Related: [UI_R002 Shiny Module ID Handling](../../CH04_ui_components/rules/UI_R002_shiny_module_id_handling.qmd), [DEV_R030 Explicit Namespace in Shiny](DEV_R030_explicit_namespace_in_shiny.qmd)*
